User-agent: *
Content-Signal: ai-train=no, search=yes, ai-input=yes
Disallow: /4.10/

# AI crawlers — both training bots and on-demand RAG/browse bots — must skip
# translated docs. Translations (ja, tr, pt-BR, ar) are auto-generated and
# not yet the source of truth; only the English content (root, /7.x/, /5.x/)
# should be ingested by AI. Trade-off: AI-search engines (OAI-SearchBot,
# PerplexityBot, etc.) also lose visibility into translated pages — that's
# the simpler-policy compromise we chose over a per-bot-role split.
# Per RFC 9309, a User-agent that matches a specific group uses ONLY that
# group's rules, so we re-declare /4.10/ and the (still-yes) Content-Signal
# for the English paths that remain Allowed.
User-agent: GPTBot
User-agent: ChatGPT-User
User-agent: OAI-SearchBot
User-agent: ClaudeBot
User-agent: Claude-Web
User-agent: anthropic-ai
User-agent: Google-Extended
User-agent: PerplexityBot
User-agent: Perplexity-User
User-agent: CCBot
User-agent: Applebot-Extended
User-agent: Bytespider
User-agent: cohere-ai
User-agent: meta-externalagent
User-agent: FacebookBot
User-agent: Diffbot
Content-Signal: ai-train=no, search=yes, ai-input=yes
Disallow: /4.10/
Disallow: /ja/
Disallow: /tr/
Disallow: /pt-BR/
Disallow: /ar/
